title: Assessing Primo Brands (PRMB) Valuation After Recent Share Price Volatility
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Primo Brands stock: recent moves in context

Primo Brands (PRMB) shares recently reflected mixed momentum, with a small 1 day decline, a gain over the past week, and a double digit drop over the past month. This has kept short term performance in focus for investors.

See our latest analysis for Primo Brands.

At a share price of US$18.72, Primo Brands has had a mixed run, with a 30 day share price return of a 14.4% decline contrasting with a 90 day gain of 15.63% and a 1 year total shareholder return of a 42.82% decline, suggesting recent momentum has cooled after earlier strength.

If this kind of volatility has you thinking about where else growth and risk are being repriced, it could be a good moment to scan 20 top founder-led companies

With PRMB trading at US$18.72 and sitting at a sizeable discount to the US$26.50 analyst target, as well as a 70.44% gap to one intrinsic estimate, is this a genuine mispricing or is the market already factoring in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 30.4% Undervalued

On the widely followed narrative, Primo Brands screens as undervalued, with a fair value of $26.88 against the recent $18.72 share price, and a 6.96% discount rate underpinning that view.

Expanding production capacity for high growth premium brands like Mountain Valley and Saratoga through new facilities in Arkansas and Texas may help unlock constrained demand and support faster revenue growth and higher mix driven margins.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what kind of revenue mix, margin rebuild, and earnings ramp this narrative is baking in? The full story links those moving parts directly to that $26.88 fair value and beyond.

Result: Fair Value of $26.88 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this hinges on smoother integration and a healthier home and office delivery network. Prolonged execution issues or weaker customer recovery could quickly challenge that undervalued story.

Find out about the key risks to this Primo Brands narrative.

Another View: Valuation Check Using Earnings

The discount to fair value looks large, yet on earnings PRMB trades on a P/E of 84.6x, versus a fair ratio of 42x, a peer average of 53.8x and a global Beverage average of 16.7x. That is a wide gap, so is the real risk that expectations are simply too high?
